evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot clearly shows up/down/non-sig classifications with threshold dashed lines and top-5 gene labels; brief's scientific claim fully realized.
evidence.typography: Script does NOT set pdf.fonttype=42; PDF embeds Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans fonts (confirmed via pdffonts), which are not editable/selectable TrueType/Type1 glyphs.
evidence.palette: Uses colorblind-distinguishable palette (#4477AA blue for down, #CC3311 red for up, grey non-sig); red/blue directional discipline is appropriate, but red/green reserved-for-direction rule is satisfied (no green used).
evidence.layout_density: 6x5 inch figure uses space well; scatter fills the panel, legend placed upper-left without overlap, top-5 labels with white stroke background avoid clutter.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption text matches figure; legend present; dashed threshold lines labeled implicitly via caption; minor concern: index-based gene labels (G1, G5…) may draw reviewer comment about lack of real identifiers, noted honestly in caption.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 fonts (not Type 1/TrueType); scatter points are rasterized (rasterized=True); text is not selectable. fonttype=42 not set in script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial (PDF ~175 KB, PNG ~94 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains axes, color coding with exact counts (up n=235, down n=230, non-sig n=2535), threshold values, and explicitly notes index-based placeholder labels — a specific numerical finding is provided.

overall_strengths: Clear, publication-ready volcano plot with correct directional color discipline, threshold lines, and top-5 gene annotation.
Excellent caption with precise numerical counts (n=235 up, n=230 down, n=2535 non-sig) and honest disclosure of placeholder labels.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 not set: PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap DejaVuSans fonts instead of editable TrueType/Type 1 glyphs, making text non-selectable and non-editable in the PDF.
Scatter points rendered as raster (rasterized=True) which is acceptable for 3000 points but combined with Type 3 fonts means the PDF is not fully vector-fidelity.
